United States.title: Federal Judge Temporarily Blocks Abortion Ban in Alabama
United States.summary: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a near-total abortion ban in Alabama, which had been enacted after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade. The ban was set to take effect immediately, but the judge's order halts its enforcement pending further litigation.

United States.title: Trump Organization Convicted in Tax Evasion Trial
United States.summary: A jury in New York has convicted the Trump Organization of 17 tax-related charges, including conspiracy and falsifying business records. The conviction is a major blow to former President Donald Trump's business empire and raises questions about his personal finances.

United States.title: US Government Reports Record High Border Apprehensions
United States.summary: The US government has reported a record high number of border apprehensions, with over 239,000 migrant encounters in June alone. The surge in border crossings has sparked concerns about national security and the capacity of US immigration authorities.

South Korea.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile
South Korea.summary: On July 7, 2024, North Korea fired a ballistic missile into the East Sea, prompting a strong warning from the US and South Korea. The missile landed in the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) of South Korea, causing concerns about the escalating tensions between the two countries.

South Korea.title: Protests erupt in Seoul over labor reform
South Korea.summary: On July 10, 2024, thousands of protesters took to the streets in Seoul to demonstrate against the government's proposed labor reform bill, which they claim will undermine workers' rights. The protests turned violent, with several police officers and protesters injured.

South Korea.title: Corruption scandal rocks South Korean government
South Korea.summary: On July 12, 2024, a high-ranking government official was arrested on corruption charges, sparking a major scandal that has shaken the confidence of the public. The official is accused of accepting bribes from a business conglomerate, which has been implicated in various scandals over the years.

Japan.title: North Korea Tests New ICBM in Response to Joint US-Japan Military Drills
Japanese Government Cracks Down on Protests Against US Military Bases
Typhoon Hinnamor Brings Widespread Flooding and Power Outages to Japan
